prompt:
We access the fleet-management portal for our maintenance-scheduling jobs, and every team rolled their own auth loop. One sends credentials on every request, one never retries after a session expires, and one keeps looping on a bad password until someone notices the lockout alert. I want one shared sessionclient.py that all of them adopt — stdlib only. test_sessionclient.py is the acceptance contract (scripted mock portal on loopback; `python3 test_sessionclient.py` green means done; test file read-only).

Note: py-cookie-sessions (existing seed) is the SERVER side of a cookie-session store — this is the CLIENT side, building the session-state machine that drives requests against such a server.

ClientState: an enum (from enum import Enum) with three members — ANONYMOUS (no session), AUTHENTICATED (session token in cookie jar, not yet expired), EXPIRED (clock has passed the lifetime). state is a property; the class drives transitions.

SessionClient(base_url, username, password, *, clock=time.monotonic)
  - login() — POST /session with JSON {"username":...,"password":...}, Content-Type application/json. On 200: extract the session token from the Set-Cookie header (name portal_sid), store it in the cookie jar, and compute the expiry deadline as clock() + expires_in if expires_in is present in the body (int, seconds); if the key is absent, the session has no proactive deadline. On any non-200: raise LoginError with the response body detail. On 200 with no Set-Cookie: raise LoginError. A failed login must leave state ANONYMOUS.
  - logout() — if ANONYMOUS, return immediately (no request). Otherwise DELETE /session with the current cookie, then clear the jar and set state ANONYMOUS regardless of the response status.
  - get_json(path) — GET base_url+path with the Authorization cookie ("Cookie: portal_sid=<token>"). Before dispatching, check whether the stored token is still valid: if state is EXPIRED, call login() first to refresh. If there is no session at all (ANONYMOUS), call login() lazily. On 401: exactly once, call login() and replay the request with the new token. On a second 401: raise AuthError (a subclass of ApiError with .status=401) and reset state to ANONYMOUS. On any other non-2xx: raise ApiError with .status = status_code. Replay the SAME path on a 401, not a redirect.
  - If a resource response carries a Set-Cookie for portal_sid, update the jar and reset the expiry deadline from the new cookie's response body (if any; otherwise leave the existing deadline).
  - LoginError is separate from ApiError; it means config or credentials trouble, not an in-flight request failure. AuthError is a subclass of ApiError. No retries, no backoff, no sleep anywhere in this module.

The clock is injectable (default time.monotonic) so tests can advance time without sleeping. The deadline comparison is: at clock() >= deadline the session is EXPIRED — the boundary itself re-logins.
